entry.title: Upcoming Enhancements to Offer Letters
entry.content:
We would like more information about the enhancements to offer letters please. 

  • What does this look like? 
  • How will it be deployed? 
  • What does configuration look like from the customer standpoint?

1H 2024 Product Release

entry.title: ATC and SAP Buffered Table
entry.content:
Hello Experts, I recently started using ATC checks to make my application perfect. Right now one of the warnings is like:

SELECT statements that bypass the SAP table buffer

 

SELECT tgsb~gsber, gtext FROM tgsb JOIN tgsbt ON tgsb~gsber EQ tgsbt~gsber 
INTO CORRESPONDING FIELDS OF TABLE @main->it_gsber WHERE spras EQ @SY-langu.

 

my code is:I need to select gsber and its name from tgsb and tgsbt do you have how can I make this select better not to bypass buffer?

entry.title: Re: Enhancing S/4HANA with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Store and GenAI
entry.content:
Fantastic! cant wait to have my hands on it!

@Gunter is there any way to log in not providing the user and the password to Hana Cloud? Oauth maybe?

entry.title: Re: March Developer Challenge - CloudEvents: It's a wrap!
entry.content: Congratulations on all who completed the March Developer Challenge! Well done 👏🎉

entry.title: Re: access odata authentication issue - power automate
entry.content:
Hi @daniellemaree,

Did you try logging in the system via automation as well? I suspect that you are either not sending all of the relevant information to the service interface that is required to perform logon (like sap-client = 002). 

Please read the following tutorial for all required attributes:

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/cp-portal-cloud-foundry-gateway-connection.html

Once you are successfully authenticated, you should receive a cookie in the request named MYSAPSSO2 - which can and should be used for any further requests to this system - to avoid doble authentications, hence optimizing it for performance. 

Hope this helps.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Change Section Height at Runtime
entry.content:
Crystal Reports throws a COMException if an element in this section would be cut off by the reduced section height.
You must include the position and size of the lowest element in the section when calculating the minimum section height.

entry.title: Need Out-of-the-box reports for Treasury
entry.content: I need the list of Out-of-the-box reports for Treasury.

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

May I ask your areas of interest and where you have or plan to obtain product expertise?

Be sure to JOIN interest groups to stay engaged. Interest Groups | SAP Community Best of luck.

Do we have any specific reason why we are using the Index or the Index field is missing in XSLT mapping.

Kindly need your help to resolve this issue.

entry.title: Re: Classical reports performance guidelines
entry.content: Please do not post a solution. Instead, click on "show replies" then "comment".

entry.title: Re: Template for Uploading NEW Role in "Maintain Business...
entry.content:
Root Cause of this issue is - Length of the Role . Maximum length shall be 40 Characters. If it more than 40 then we get PARSING error.

Regards

Sreekanth Peram

entry.title: Knowledge Graphs on Datasphere and HANA Cloud. The differences
entry.content:
The ability to enhance RAG with Vector search, Graph knowledge bases, or SQL Queries is fundamental. SAP recently announced the Datasphere capability to create Graphs, and there are some key differences between the graph capabilities in SAP HANA Cloud, the new announcement SAP Datasphere knowledge graph and the Intergation Suite capability called Graph:

1. SAP HANA Cloud has had a powerful graph engine for analyzing complex relationships in business data for a while now. It allows creating graph workspaces from existing tables/views, querying graphs using openCypher, writing custom graph algorithms, and visualizing graphs.

 

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-graph-resources/ba-p/13505490

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=oaSQu9laYKE

2. Datasphere Creating knowledge graphs manually is very costly, so SAP Datasphere comes to help by automatically pre-populating them using content already available in SAP Datasphere like connections to source systems, tables, foreign keys etc. SAP Datasphere knowledge graph is a new capability announced in March 2024. As data is onboarded and integrated into SAP Datasphere, it automatically creates an ontology representing the relationships in the data, including business context from SAP sources like S/4HANA

caption by SAP. Currently in Preview

 

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=PQrFjthwOWQ&t=169

https://news.sap.com/2024/03/future-of-data-driven-business-new-sap-datasphere-capabilities/

3. SAP Graph as an Integration Suite capability 

Parallel to building Graphs from Databases, SAP Graph is an alternative unified API available on the SAP Business Technology Platform that provides access to a connected graph of SAP-managed business data, regardless of where the data resides.

SAP Graph is intended for building extension applications, such as workflow applications using low-code tools or event-driven applications, and it supports modern open standards like OData v4 and GraphQL

 

 

https://podcast.opensap.info/cloud-platform/2022/04/28/episode-82-sap-graph-the-easy-to-use-api-of-the-intelligent-enterprise/

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/graph-new-in-sap-integration-suite/ba-p/13555887

Conclusion

While the SAP HANA Cloud graph is more for custom graph algorithm development and analysis, the SAP Datasphere knowledge graph is an automated capability that provides a rich context for generative AI use cases by capturing complex data relationships and metadata. The two serve different but complementary purposes in the SAP data and analytics portfolio.

Additional information:
https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/how-use-sap-hana-cloud-graph-model-analyze-complex-your-holitschke

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-graph-resources/ba-p/13505490

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/native-hana-vs-sap-datasphere-findings/qaq-p/12689997

https://www.techzine.eu/blogs/data-management/117331/sap-expands-datasphere-with-knowledge-graph-and...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-datasphere-is-an-alternative-of-sap-hana-modelling-w...

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/access-sap-hana-cloud-underneath-of-sap-dat...

https://developers.sap.com/group.hana-aa-graph-overview.html

entry.title: Re: its possible create SAP notes?
entry.content:
SAP notes are only provided by SAP.

If you need to make changes or corrections, you have the TMS functionality (create order, release and then import).

If you need to transport the correction for other systems not belonging to the TMS, you need to copy the DATA and COFILE files to the same folders on SYSTEM destination and then add them to the queue.

entry.title: Re: Build Apps Import Project
entry.content:
Hi Vishal,

I faced the same challenge when converting from AppGyver to SAP Build.

However, It's easy.

All you have to do is Export your project from appgyver 

 

and then import the project in your SAP Build Lobby

 

and then Upload your project file and select the record and click import.

 

Then your project will be created and usable as any normal Build Apps. 

 

Hope you find my steps useful 😊

entry.title: Re: Automatic creation of the job info and compensation p...
entry.content:
Hi Manish Jaiswal

Thanks for your response.

Do you know where can i get some documentation?

Regards

entry.title: Re: ATC and SAP Buffered Table
entry.content: From SELECT online documentation you could find that JOIN expressions currently always bypass the buffer. So you could remove the join, actually you don't need it as every field is available in the text table (exception: if some translations are missing, but you didn't use an explicit OUTER JOIN)

entry.title: How to use CDS View in Calculation View?
entry.content:
Hi, 

I want to create a new Calculation View with a CDS View named CSTOCKQUANVALCUR, but I cannot create a Virtual Table because I think CSTOCKQUANVALCUR needs parameters.

That's why I looked at the view that is the source of CSTOCKQUANVALCUR and saw that the CDS View named 'PMATSTKQTYVALCU1' has no parameters. Also, when I checked, I saw that I could create a Virtual Table with this CDS View.

After creating the Virtual Table, I selected this CDS View in SQL Console, but I saw that no data came. I selected this View directly in the Source System instead of Virtual Table, but no data came again.

However, when I do Data Preview in ABAP or look at SE11/SE16N, I see data coming.

My question is this: How can I use this CDS View in Calculation View? If there is an authorization problem, how can I pass it?

Kind regards
Margit

But getting the below issue.

TU for delivery assignment / not found

But getting below issue

Transport planning data of TU 20000402 and delivery 80000479 do not match

How to proceed further.

entry.title: Read RAP Service response headers sap-message in CAPM NodeJS based application
entry.content:
Dear all,

In a NodeJS based CAPM application, we are making a RAP service call to the S4 on-premise service to create a Posting document. The RAP service is sending the newly created document number in the response header and not in the response object itseldf.

Here in the 'result' object, we do not see the new accounting document as RAP service is unable to send it but it does send the same in the response header -> field 'sap-message' as shown here

 

How can I access the same in the NodeJS 'result' object as shown in the code.

Regards,

Nilesh

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration – Connecting to Messaging Systems using the AMQP Adapter
entry.content:
If you don't see a difference, probably the amount of messages in the queues is not large enough? Is there a real backlog in the queue that needs to be handled? If so and there is no upscaling of the processes even if configured I would suggest you open a ticket on LOD-HCI-PI-CON-SOAP

Thank you

Mandy

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@jenifer_ranjani 
From what I can see, the account with which you posted this reply is actually your older account, from 2022.

Where exactly are you trying to create the blog post? Regardless of rank, you should be able to submit a blog post in a group. And since you have Participant rank (see more about ranks here https://community.sap.com/t5/what-s-new/let-the-gamification-begin/ba-p/311892), you also have authorization to submit a blog post in the Product & Technology categories.

I would try logging out and logging in again, then see if you still get the error when you try to post. If you do, please write an email to community@sap.com and the team will investigate the issue.

Regards,
Audrey

Many Thanks,
Harsh

entry.title: Fiscal Year
entry.content:
If a company follows FY as calendar year and company’s month closing dates follows as 28,28,35 days method for all months ( example Jan - 28 days , Feb - 28 days , Mar - 35 days and so on) Please let me know how will you define (in OB29) FY in SAP in S4 Hana. Here find below the financial calendar. 

MonthFromTo
101/01/202428/01/2024
229/01/202425/02/2024
326/02/202431/03/2024
401/04/202428/04/2024
530/04/202427/05/2024
628/05/202401/07/2024
702/07/202429/07/2024
830/07/202426/08/2024
927/08/202430/09/2024
1001/10/202428/10/2024
1129/10/202425/11/2024
1226/11/202430/12/2024

entry.title: Return order should contain original items only
entry.content:
Requirement: Restrict the addition of new material line items in Return Sales Order that was created with reference to an existing original Invoice. Restrict the increase in quantity of the original items.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Return order should contain original items only
entry.content: It may be possible to create a extensibility implementation. For example, you could try creating two custom fields - YY1_ORIGINAL, YY1_ORIGINALQTY. Fill these fields either through the BAdI SD_SLS_DATA_TRANSFER_ITEM (Data Transfer for Sales Document Items) or BADI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM~MODIFY_FIELDS depending on your specific business requirement. Then create a check and raise an error message in BADI - SD_SLS_CHECK_ITEM - checking for a qty change against yy1_original or a new item where YY1_ORIGINAL is not equal to X.

entry.title: Re: TU for delivery assignment / not found
entry.content: Please Verify the Transportation Planning Type in Delivery Header. It controls the behaviour of manual TU creation and assignment. (You can control the behaviour per document type in SPRO => Define transportation Planning type

entry.title: Re: SAP IBP: Import Purchasing Data from Excel Files and Load into Order Based Planning using Python
entry.content:
Could you please share details of the dataset j5 used in this commit statement?

if x.status_code == 201:
#Commit the POST Request
    y = requests.post(f"{DATA_URL}/SAP__self.Commit",data=j5,auth=(USERNAME, PASSWORD), verify=False, headers = headers, cookies = c )
​

 We are getting the error of incompatible data type.

Thanks,

Anishka Moona

entry.title: Re: its possible create SAP notes?
entry.content:
Only SAP (and some partners) can provide SAP notes 

You can create a SAR file with command sapcar -cvf, look at blogs such as Transporting ABAP Objects from one Landscape to Another, and send it to customers.

entry.title: Re: Error during oData API_CV_ATTACHMENT_SRV in SAP Integ...
entry.content:
Hi @Simon,

OData adapter in Cloud Integration has to be setup as XML document. If you send as payload a JSON string, it will cause that error.

https://me.sap.com/notes/0003074490

Please see the note statements here:

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/configure-odata-v2-receiver-adapter

 It is also valid for OData v4.

The note states that you must treat all payload in an iflow as XML and once it gets sent out to a backend server that requires JSON string as payload it will be converted internally by the adapter. 

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Result recording is not updating/Overwriting the batc...
entry.content: Hi, you are welcome. Have you also checked whether there are any custom implementations which can cause this issue? Please refer the KBA 3402151.

entry.title: How to setup/configure NWDI in SAP MII for Content Development
entry.content:
This document will guide you through all steps necessary for the initial configuration of NetWeaver Development Infrastructure (NWDI) and its integration with SAP MII. After all configuration steps are done, you will be able to enable NWDI feature via SAP MII Home Page and use NWDI powered processes.

Please check the attached document to guide you through the complete process.

The document describes two systems:

1) Source System: where SLD and NWDI server is configured.

2) Target System: where MII runs.

MII and NWDI Configuration Guide 

Once the configuration is completed, you should be able to enable the NWDI and use MII along with NWDI.

entry.title: eDocument Romania - Supplier Invoice - SOAP:1033 Operation [...] not supported
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

We are configuring eDocument Document and Reporting Compliance for Romania. We have issue with the Supplier Invoices.

If we run report EDOC_MESSAGE_HANDLING with message type RO_MESSAGE_PULL we receive the following error

SOAP:1033 <no error message available>: ("Operation "PagesList{http://www.sap.com/eDocument/Romania/einvoice/v1.0}" not supported (interface: "CO_EDO_RO_E_DOCUMENT_RO" binding key: "0050569E2B411EEEA8

Message no. EDOCUMENT000

We checked and all prerequisite SAP notes seem to be already installed on the system. The troubleshooting note 3405142 does not cover this error. 

Has anyone experienced the same?

Thank you 
Regards

entry.title: SAP HCM and ME-Doc integration
entry.content:
Hello colleagues,

Does SAP HCM have any integration with a regulatory reporting service, such as ME-Doc? If not, are there any community-made options?

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Sample code for creating a responsive popover on hove...
entry.content:
Hi @ashibedi,

How about using the "tooltip" attribute of an image component in a Table Cell, like an Icon?

Here is a sample:

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/entity/sap.m.ImageContent/sample/sap.m.sample.ImageContent

Here is a sample you can use for a Popover button in UI5:

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/#/entity/sap.m.Popover/sample/sap.m.sample.Popover/code

Then you could extend the button with the hover functionality to display what you want:

https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/d5b756bf4e9a4d67961fa21e1ba12c9e 

You could adapt the code to your needs while displaying an Icon on a Table Cell. 

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Introduction
entry.content:
Welcome @Sahil_Jain great that you are part of SAP Community now and we are looking forward to reading your learnings once shared. For writing blog posts, please check and conduct the Tour the Community Tutorial first 😊

Please also consider some time to get blog posts published, e.g. when you want to post it in a group, you need to wait for a curator to get it published.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Gerhard,

Currently as I understand based on the Incoterms stage building 2 stages are created in the FU e.g. Delivering plant to Port of loading and Port of Loading to receiving plant (depending on the Incoterms).

But if the requirement here is to split the FU into 3 stages i.e 1. Delivering plant to Port of Loading 2. Port of loading to Port of Discharge 3. Port of Discharge to Receiving plant to capture since we can have different carriers managing Pre-carriage leg, Main-Carriage leg, and On-Carriage leg. Is there a way to manage such scenario in Advanced Intercompany STO process? Or is it going to be a manual activity? 

Thanks

entry.title: Warning message : Duplicate check not possible; required fields Tax number not filled
entry.content:
Hi Experts ,

We are encountering a warning message " Duplicate check not possible; required fields Tax number not filled "  even though post populating the tax number along with tax category. Can anyone help why this warning message is popped up and how to resolve this issue.

Thanks & Regards, 

Resh

entry.title: Re: its possible create SAP notes?
entry.content:
Hi @caio_dh_consultig,

I believe what you are looking for is a way to deliver your software to SAP customers in such a way that they would be able to install it with ease. 

SAP Notes contain attachments and are created by SAP only (as mentioned here already) as means to distribute software corrections that can be applied via SNOTE transaction. 

However, you can use Add-on Assembly Kit (AAK) to create your own software and distribute to customer so they can easily install and uninstall it from their SAP instances using standard Add-On Component Management transactions such as SAINT:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ADD-ON_ASSEMBLY_KIT

That approach is the correct way to distribute any ABAP code to customers as it clearly demands your code to have a namespace defined and standard and known procedures to get it installed/uninstalled.  And, in order to achieve this, you ought to certify your add-on with SAP. This is all explained on the documentation I linked here.

Some Consulting Firms will grab a Transport Request directly from the filesystem and send it to a customer to be imported into their own system. That should be OK as long as the customer understands that the ABAP code contained in that TR will have no relation to SAP and it is considered on "Customer's Domain" - usually assigned to a 'Z' or 'Y' namespace that cannot be traced back to who originally code it.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
@audreystevenson 

Thank you for your response : )

I have cleared the cache and re-logged in.. still the same, when I click on "Create a blog". I have also sent an email now to the ID which you have shared.

BR,

Jenifer

Regard,

Nguyen Mai Nhu Luan

entry.title: Enhancing Performance in SAP Web Applications: Strategies and Best Practices
entry.content:
Understanding the Performance Landscape:

Performance in SAP web applications is a multifaceted issue, encompassing aspects such as response time, data processing efficiency, and user interface fluidity. The complexity of SAP systems, coupled with the demand for high-speed data processing and real-time analytics, makes performance optimization a critical task for developers and administrators.

Key Strategies for Optimization:

  • Efficient Use of SAP Fiori and UI5: SAP Fiori and SAP UI5 are at the forefront of SAP’s user experience. To optimize performance, it’s essential to minimize the number of round-trips between the client and server, use asynchronous calls judiciously, and leverage caching mechanisms effectively.
  • Optimizing SAP HANA Usage: SAP HANA's in-memory computing capabilities offer significant performance benefits. To fully leverage these, developers should optimize data models, use proper indexing, and ensure efficient query design to minimize data processing times.
  • Code Optimization: In any SAP web application, clean and efficient coding practices are paramount. This includes minimizing unnecessary database calls, optimizing loops and internal tables, and adhering to SAP’s best coding practices.
  • Load Balancing and Scalability: Implementing load balancing techniques and ensuring the application is scalable to handle increased load are vital. This involves balancing the load across servers and optimizing resource allocation.
  • Effective Use of Caching: Caching frequently accessed data reduces the number of database hits, significantly improving response times. Appropriate use of browser and server-side caching can lead to substantial performance gains.
  • Regular Performance Testing: Conducting regular performance testing, including stress and load testing, helps in identifying bottlenecks and areas for improvement. Tools like SAP Load Runner can be instrumental in this process.

Best Practices for SAP Web Application Performance:

  • Monitor Regularly: Continuous monitoring of application performance is essential. Tools like SAP Solution Manager and SAP Fiori Monitor can provide valuable insights into application performance metrics.
  • Optimize Network Performance: Ensure that the network configuration is optimized for the best performance. This includes looking at aspects like bandwidth, latency, and data packet sizes.
  • Responsive Design Considerations: For mobile or responsive web applications, performance optimization also includes UI elements that are lightweight and adaptable to different screen sizes without compromising on performance.
  • User Experience Focus: A performant application is not just about speed. It’s also about how quickly and efficiently users can complete their tasks. This means optimizing navigation flows, search functionalities, and overall application layout.
  • Cloud Integration: When using SAP Cloud Platform, leverage cloud capabilities for better performance. This includes utilizing cloud-based processing power and storage effectively.

Performance optimization in SAP web applications is a continuous process, not a one-time fix. As technology evolves and business needs change, strategies for optimization must also adapt. By focusing on efficient coding practices, leveraging SAP's advanced tools, and maintaining a user-centric approach, organizations can significantly enhance the performance and user experience of their SAP web applications.

entry.title: Re: MRP: Schedule Lines

entry.title: Re: eDocument Romania - Supplier Invoice - SOAP:1033 Oper...
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

leaving the solution I found myself with a troubleshooting note of another country (Mexico)...

the logical port of the involved consumer proxy must be deleted and re-created, identical, but this time in the operation lists the new operations will show up. 

Hope this helps! 

Thank you
Regards

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 0
Re: Introduction

thank you so much!

entry.title: Re: How can I find out what version of RAP I am using
entry.content:
Hi @BODHISATTWA__20,

RAP is simply a framework that has to be followed when creating extensions on an S/4HANA system. 

The important fact here is to ensure the development model used is ABAP Cloud.

Please read more:

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/abap/abap-cloud-faq

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/make-use-of-abap-cloud-support-in-the-abap-development-tools-for-eclipse/ba-p/13558622

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-check-your-custom-code-for-abap-cloud/ba-p/13567124

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: How to deploy SAPUI5 apps on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content:
Hello @Ivan-Mirisola,

I understand that for developer extensibility in an #SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud environment, it is achieved using Core Data Services to provision data, and for front-end applications, we can do it with Fiori Elements. However, I'm not entirely clear if it's possible to develop a pro code freestyle application and deploy it in an S/4HANA Public Cloud environment.

Additionally, I would like to know the step-by-step process of configuring the connection between BTP and S/4HANA Public Cloud, from setting up the communication scenario, configuring the communication user, to creating the destination. Typically, the step of creating the communication scenario is skipped, and the destination creation is done with the scenario data. For example, I have the following case:

  1. I downloaded the certificate from the Security/Destinations section in BTP.
  2. I created a communication scenario in the S/4HANA Public Cloud system and uploaded the certificate in SAML.
  3. I configured a destination to the ERP API.
  4. I tried to deploy the Fiori application using the destination but couldn't do it.

Your assistance would be greatly appreciated as this is an important project for the region.

Regards, Anthony

entry.title: Re: Unleashing AI and Machine Learning in Sales: Advanced Price-Volume Forecasting with SAP Analytic
entry.content:
Hi Dirk,

Great post to illustrate how a predictive forecast can incorporate price as an influencer!

My next questions would now be:
1) Regarding 'optimized price planning': Based on the planned volumes, what is the optimal price to reach highest revenue (optimization problem)?
2) Regarding 'reducing manual effort': Currently SAC planners need to copy the price into each forecast period granularity, and into the same model. Can this activity be avoided? (e.g. by using an easy valid from/to date like in ECC)

entry.title: Re: its possible create SAP notes?
entry.content: I'm sorry to insist, but this notes will not edit standard reports, they will create z products.

entry.title: multi-day absence entry
entry.content: Currently, multi-day absence is keyed in as one line with a start date and end date. This creates challenges to report absences that span across multiple weeks, months, and years for a clean weekly, monthly and yearly report. A lot of data clean-up needs to be done to declutter the days first. Is it possible to change the configuration to single day entry?

Hi @ajmaradiaga 

Is there any chance this functionality somehow got broken? During the last days, I noticed that my feed reader (Feedly) suddenly started to show a ton of entries per tag:

Regards,

entry.title: Send Approval Form to a user in different tenant
entry.content: Can we send approval forms to a user in different tenant in SAP BPA.

entry.title: Navigating OData Service Customization in ABAP Cloud
entry.content:
Hello everyone!

I'm engaged in a project on SAP BTP ABAP environment and encountering challenges with OData services. Traditionally, in the on-premises ABAP environment, we could utilize the SEGW transaction to create and adjust OData services, including renaming entities to meet our naming standards or needs. However, SEGW is not accessible in the SAP BTP ABAP environment, where RAP has become the preferred technology. I'm exploring alternative methods to achieve similar outcomes.

One of our goals is to rename navigation properties (associations) in OData services generated from referenced CDS views. I aim to align the names of navigation properties in OData services with our project's naming guidelines, preferring "Entity" over the usual "toEntity".

Additionally, I've encountered issues with reserved words, such as CDS fields, in the SAP BTP ABAP environment, which were not problematic in on-premises settings (link).

The reason I wish to keep the services identical (at a metadata level) in SAP BTP ABAP environment and on-premises is to connect the new cloud OData services to existing development resources, such as Fiori applications and third-party integrations.

Creative solutions or indirect methods may be necessary due to these limitations. Any advice, tips, or documentation references would be highly valued.

Looking forward to your insights and suggestions.

Thank you!

Rafael

entry.title: Logs not getting printed for users having name length greater than 50 characters
entry.content:
Logs are not being printed for users with names exceeding 50 characters. Upon investigating, it was discovered that the column size for the 'User' in the Logs table is set to 50 characters, thus limiting the logging for longer names.



------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Logs not getting printed for users having name length...
entry.content:
This action won't be logged in the event log; instead, it will solely store data in memory without any log record. To register information in the log, you must utilize the log.write method. This method offers various implementations, differing only in the number of parameters used. Similarly, the trace.write method places records in the developer console.

In essence, the key distinction between the developer console and the event log lies in data retention. The event log ensures permanent storage by storing data in a database table for up to two weeks, whereas the developer console does not utilize a database and thus cannot retain data as part of the event log. Therefore, utilizing the log.write methods remains the sole method to store information in the event log.

entry.title: Applicant Status Configuration Export
entry.content: Is there any way to Export the configured Applicant Status Sets from STG to PRODUCTION? At the moment, we see we have to do manually in Provisioning to bring the new statuses in and also Recruiters have to pull the needed ones into status sets same as their STG Configuration. Is there any easy way to export along with Grouping of a status, required, other selections of status attributes in a status set either from Provisioning or from Front end by Recruiters? Or any way in Integration Center to get all this.

entry.title: Removing table/canvas reports with Stories being released
entry.content: Just wondering if it is really necessary to remove Table or Canvas reports with Stories being released. They are being used to meet different reporting needs and have different use cases. What is the rationale for only keeping Stories in the near future.

entry.title: Re: Story Report Filter Error
entry.content: I am having these same problems now in 2024 - Any advice? I am trying to learn Stories for the big change in Nov 2025 but this is just so frustrating. If it's not one error code, it's another or the system won't even load the report. It's basically a large table report - No graphs or special data analysis, just fields.

entry.title: Re: April (Citizen) Developer Challenge – SAP Build Apps: Task 0
Re: SAP blog awaiting review

entry.title: Re: Creating Pay Component in One Time Portlet using Integration Center
entry.content:
Hi lsharma

Thanks for your post

we have the following requirement:

Automatic creation of the job info and compensation portlet in Success Factors

The compensation portlet must be updated with the next pay scale level

The new salary structure (pay scale type, pay scale area, pay scale group and pay scale level) must be updated in the job information portlet

These are the conditions:

The employee must have been working in the company for 3 months.

The employee must have completed the mandatory courses for newly hired employees, This information is in a custom portlet.

We need to create an integration that runs every Friday to update the job information and compensation portlet for eligible employees.

The company has around 25000 employees

Can we solve the requiriment with integration center?

Thanks in advance 

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: SAP blog awaiting review
entry.content: Hi @Nitin_chandel - the moderation team has been notified. If something like this happens again, it is quicker to send an email to community@sap.com. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to deploy SAPUI5 apps on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content:
Hi @ivan

 

I have build my rap app using Eclipse. I understand that is required BAS to create sapui5(BSP app). This app must deploy from SAP BAS to S4 HANA Public Edition, but firstly I should be establish Trust Configuration between SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition and SAP BTP Subaccount. This is correct?.

A lot of tutorial I find the same case, but I cannot deploy fiori app from SAP BAS to S4 Hana.

Could you help with this question?

Follow this tutorial;

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-custom-ui-bas-connect-s4hc.html

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-sdm-gwi-s4hanacloud-export.html

entry.title: How to change a item from make to buy? When costed it is still putting in labor $$
entry.content: I have a item in one of my plants which is manufacturing a part. It is selling it to the sister plant. This should go at cost to the sister facility. However when the cost roll is done at the sister plant the material is not being picked up correctly and there is also labor coming in as well. The price was loaded into the sister facility and there is no router and BOM. The flag is also set up as a buy item. Any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud – SAP S/4HANA extensibility – May 2023 update
entry.content:
Hi Boris,

If you could provide us with your great support with those questions.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-deploy-sapui5-apps-on-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition/qaq-p/13654859#M4823723

Regards,
Anthony Ramos

entry.title: Data Load errors during the recent Daylight Saving Time (DST)
entry.content:
Our BW system (SAP BW 7.5 SP 19 on DB6) in EST ran into errors during the recent Daylight Saving Time (DST) change on March 11, 2024. We see errors with "MESSAGE_TYPE_X," "SAPLRS_GENERAL" (or LRS_GENERALU05), and "RSSM_GET_TIME" in the ST22 logs. We believe it's because a data load grabbed a timestamp that was one hour behind an earlier timestamp, causing the system to wait for a non-existent time. This triggered errors when the difference exceeded six seconds.
Instead of removing data loads from the schedule, are there other ways to handle DST changes? Are there any recommended solutions from SAP?

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Kolkata 2024
entry.content:
Hi ,

Please let me know how I can attend this event.

entry.title: Data Load errors during the recent Daylight Saving Time (DST).
entry.content:
Our BW system (SAP BW 7.5 SP 19 on DB6) in EST ran into errors during the recent Daylight Saving Time (DST) change on March 11, 2024. We see errors with "MESSAGE_TYPE_X," "SAPLRS_GENERAL" (or LRS_GENERALU05), and "RSSM_GET_TIME" in the ST22 logs. We believe it's because a data load grabbed a timestamp that was one hour behind an earlier timestamp, causing the system to wait for a non-existent time. This triggered errors when the difference exceeded six seconds.
Instead of removing data loads from the schedule, are there other ways to handle DST changes? Are there any recommended solutions from SAP?

entry.title: Re: SAP Inside Track Kolkata 2024
entry.content:
Hello 
Kindly show your interest in the top right option. Please join the Kolkata Group 

----- Stay up-to-date with SAP Community and subscribe to What's New today! -----

entry.title: Re: How to deploy SAPUI5 apps on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content: Hi

entry.title: Re: How to deploy SAPUI5 apps on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public...
entry.content:
Hi @anthonyramosr,

Which version of Public are you currently using?

Did you follow the requirements on the bellow documentation:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/6aa39f1ac05441e5a23f484f31e477e7/e3527dfa1c5a41f69d4e63691f174f0a.html?locale=en-US

The following tutorial has some prerequisites in terms of which system you are using as well as configuration work to connect BAS and S/4HANA Tenant. Which means that you will not use ADT for the fron-end.

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-s4hanacloud-procurement-purchasereq-shop.html

For a custom UI front-end deployment, you could use the following Mission:

https://developers.sap.com/group.abap-custom-ui-s4hana-cloud.html

I suspect you could develop the Fiori front-end entirely on ADT, but then you would have to understand where to place each development artifact mimicking what BAS does in the background. Either way, BAS and VSCode both do a better job at developing fron-end UIs than ADT.

Best regards,
Ivan

entry.title: Re: BAPI_SYSTEM_ALERT_GETDETAILS
entry.content: Can someone share the solution and not finding much information or resolutions from sap notes so far.

entry.title: How to create custom OData service in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hello Support,
We have a Query regarding a Custom Data Source.
We got a requirement for a Custom Business Scenario in which we have developed Custom CDS for Form Template but it has been observed that it is not possible to make Custom Data Source through CDS.

We tried doing this in Data Source Extensions, but it allows only the addition of fields in existing data sources.

Is there any workaround for making a Completely New Custom Data Source?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom OData service in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
It's possible to create a custom OData service using ABAP RAP with ADT against the development tenant in a 3-system landscape for S/4HANA Cloud. Please see this documentation for an example:

Developing an OData Service for Simple List Reporting | SAP Help Portal

Thanks,

SAP Support

entry.title: Assistance Needed: Issue with SAP B1 Version 10 Hana Invoice Series Display
entry.content:
Hello All Members,

My name is Rahul, and I am new to this community. I have one question: we are currently using SAP B1 Version 10 Hana. As you know, the new financial year has started, and we need to create new series for it.

My query is regarding my client, who has two sales invoice series: the first is 2324, and the second is 2425. Now, when the user creates a sales invoice using the new series, they encounter an issue where both series' data are displayed. For example, 2023 already posted the first number, and 2024 posted the new invoice. So, the user requires that when they select a particular series, like 2024 invoice number, and then click on previous or next record, only invoices from 2024 should be shown, not both series.

Does anyone have a solution or any customization for this? Please let me know. Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Use SAPUI5 Single Planning Calendar but not for Appointments
entry.content:
I would like to use the Single Planning Calendar to show data I'm retrieving from an HTTP GET request.  When I went into the View.xml file and tried to add a different type of data structure, I got an error saying that the aggregation I wanted to add doesn't exist in the sap.m.SinglePlanningCalendar class.

The information I want to add instead of an appointment has a few more pieces of data than the Appointment aggregation.

I just noticed that there is another Aggregation called CustomData but I'm not sure how to use it. 

Is it possible that this might work for this project?

If so, does anyone happen to know of a tutorial or article that would show how to use it?  I've started looking but haven't found anything useful, yet.

Thanks in advance for any and all suggestions!

Cheers!

Ron

entry.title: Talent Intelligence hub universal adoption
entry.content:
Hi

Is there a date with Talent Intelligence hub is going to be required as a universal adoption item? 

Trying to understand the impact to our performance forms that use competencies so we can roll this out before any universal adoption deadline.

entry.title: Re: How to export CR to pdf by API Gateway
entry.content:
Hi Channa, I've never used this before...however, the payload below (and the payload from the API help guide) use "isOptionalPrompt": "false" and there is no value, i.e. "values": []

 

In the scheduler for SAP BI CR Server a schedule of a report, where there is a non-optional parameter and no value has been entered for that parameter, there will be an error of "Information is needed before this report can be processed." A schedule of the same report with a parameter value will be successful. In the report designer there is a similar, and expected behaviour.

 

Can you check to see if your reports do in fact have Optional Parameters or not and adjust your payload and retry so that non-optional parameters do have values. Let use know if that resolves the issue.

entry.title: Re: Delta extraction of ADSO from SAP BW bridge into SAP Datasphere via Transformation Flow
entry.content: Thanks for the clarification @wilson_kurian3

entry.title: Re: SAP UX Innovations in Public Cloud Products, Joule, AI, Entry Points, Apple Vision Pro and more
entry.content:
Dear Thomas,

Thanks for sharing these insights. Can you please share from which version of SAPUI5 and S/4HANA the feature of "Copy and Paste in SAPUI5 Tables" you have mentioned in section "Improvements in Details" will be available for S/4HANA on-premise. We are on S/4HANA 2023 FSP00 but we don't find this copy and paste feature in Fiori apps.

Thanks in advance for your response.

Regards

Sundeep

entry.title: Re: Eclipse ADT crash when accessing to Open others > Text Elements
entry.content:
SOLVED

Effectively temurin doesn't include JavaFX.

To solve :
1. Download JavaFX from https://jdk.java.net/21/ 
2. Decompress archive
3. Copy the content of /lib ( which contain lib files and the expected javafx.properties ) to the /lib folder of your java VM
4. Restart eclipse

I try with Java 17 and Java 21 both work well. So we can have different JVM from SAP GUI and Eclipse. I choosen to keep Java21.

Tips :
To modify the Java used by Eclipse edit the file eclipse.ini and change the line just after -vm

 

-vm
/Users/mac-ADUHAM28/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/temurin-21.0.1/Contents/Home/bin

 

Thanks WRoeckelein for your help!

entry.title: Re: Badi ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST Method SET_DATA not updating table
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you solve this issue? We are also facing same problem. We are trying to update custom field.

looks like we need to enhance structure MEREQ_ITEM_S_CUST_ALLOWED. Only fields of this structure are updated via SET method.

thanks

entry.title: Re: Badi ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST Method SET_DATA not updating table
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you solve this issue? We are also facing same problem. We are trying to update custom field.

looks like we need to enhance structure MEREQ_ITEM_S_CUST_ALLOWED. Only fields of this structure are updated via SET method.

entry.title: Re: Badi ME_PROCESS_REQ_CUST Method SET_DATA not updating table
entry.content:
Hi,

Did you solve this issue? We are also facing same problem. We are trying to update custom field.

looks like we need to enhance structure MEREQ_ITEM_S_CUST_ALLOWED. Only fields of this structure are updated via SET method.

entry.title: Re: Fantastic SAP AI SDKs and where to find them
entry.content:
Hi @thomas_mller13 . Yes, apologies if all this is complicated. Just imagine I tell you AI Core is the successor of Leonardo ML Foundation and would totally confuse all this.

PAL is on HANA, so SQLScript is there, but you call PAL from ADMP, which is ABAP-based. You will find more information on how to create an ML model on Tensorflow or Pytorch; the development of these platforms is more advanced due to their higher usage. But even though I am not a data scientist, I find PAL really good with HANA structured data, whereas the other libraries are not good for HANA tables. Just there. Don't expect to build an LLM on HANA. Well, unless we get a surprise from Philipp 🙂

entry.title: Re: Updating Multiple Records through the Bulk Update Functionality in Agent Performance Management
entry.content: Great post! Thanks! One of my teammates discovered the bulk change function recently and we have found multiple uses already. I would love the ability to enter a list of contracts, producers, etc. when the list is already known, like multiple contract numbers separated by a comma for example. I submitted the idea through the influence portal. Please vote if you agree this would be useful: Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence (sap.com)

entry.title: Re: SAC Story - How to show YOY % based on dynamic date s...
entry.content:
Hi, you might have a look at YoY formula:  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/e2e05ae1c521436fbc71c45208272ab9.html 

Regards,

Sarau

entry.title: Re: SAP blog awaiting review
entry.content:
Hi @Nitin_chandel and @HarshilShah - please be aware that if your blog post needs to go through moderation, the moderation team may require up to five business days to conduct a review. 
Because of Easter we have had many public holidays here in Europe, which is why it might seem to take longer than usual at the moment for reviews to occur. 

Please only send an email to community@sap.com if more than five business days have passed since you submitted the blog post for review.
